message:
fix(runtime): improve join semantics with PolledAfterCompletion and proper close handling

TaskHandle, ActorHandle, and GenServerHandle join futures all shared
the same class of bugs: they could be polled after completion, and
they conflated oneshot-channel-closed with parent-cancellation.

TaskHandle/JoinFuture:
- Rename `completed` to `terminal` (tracks future exhaustion) and
  add separate `drop_abort_defused` (for combinator control flow),
  fixing a conflation where defuse_drop_abort() set completed=true
  which suppressed the drop-abort safety net
- Add PolledAfterCompletion variant to JoinError
- Distinguish RecvError::Closed (task dropped without sending) from
  RecvError::Cancelled (Cx-level cancellation) for accurate error
  propagation

ActorHandle:
- Add `completed` flag to prevent double-join
- Replace unwrap_or_else closure with explicit match on Closed vs
  Cancelled recv errors so the actor's own cancel reason propagates
  instead of always using the caller's Cx reason
- Add OnStopMaskGuard RAII type for safe mask_depth management

GenServerHandle:
- Mirror the same completed-tracking and explicit recv-error matching
  as ActorHandle for consistency
- Add #[derive(Debug)] to Counter test helper

cx/scope.rs:
- Handle new JoinError::PolledAfterCompletion in select_biased match

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
